title: Proof of q2: Problem 2: Whittaker Functions and Rankin--Selberg Integrals

Issues must reference a concrete proof file and identify an exact location (section, lemma, equation, or quoted text) where the problem occurs. Re-open and update this issue once an AI proof attempt has been made for this problem.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Mathematical Review (critic-agent) — no proof attempt yet

There is currently **no `solution.tex`** for this problem. Per task, I analyzed the problem statement directly and created sub-lemma issues for the key steps a solver must establish. Full write-up linked as **Critic Analysis** (`questions/q2/analysis.md`).

**Problem (expected answer: YES).** $\exists W\in\mathcal W(\Pi,\psi^{-1})\ \forall\pi\ \exists V\in\mathcal W(\pi,\psi)$ with the JPSS integral $\int_{N_n\backslash\mathrm{GL}_n}W(\operatorname{diag}(g,1)u_Q)V(g)|\det g|^{s-1/2}dg$ finite and nonzero for all $s\in\mathbb C$, where $u_Q=I_{n+1}+QE_{n,n+1}$ and $|Q|=q^{a(\pi)}$.

**Key sub-lemmas (new issues):**
- **q2-6** — Existence of a single $\pi$-independent $W$ serving all $\pi$ (the $\exists W\,\forall\pi$ uniformity; conductor carried by $u_Q$).
- **q2-7** — Single determinant-shell support $\Rightarrow$ collapse to a nonzero monomial $c_0\,c^{s-1/2}$ (entire & nowhere-zero iff $c_0\ne0$). Documented FATAL danger: previous attempts got *multi-shell* support $\{|Q|\le|y|\le|Q|q^n\}$ because $1/L(s,\pi)$ is a degree-$\le n$ polynomial; killing the extra shells is the heart.
- **q2-8** — Nonvanishing $c_0\ne0$ for ALL generic $\pi$ (not just unramified/unitary), via explicit Matringe/Miyauchi newvector values + Langlands classification + analytic continuation.
- **q2-9** — Realizability of $W$ in $\mathcal W(\Pi,\psi^{-1})$ (BZ/mirabolic) and the off-mirabolic translation identities from Whittaker equivariance.
- **q2-2 (existing)** — Misuse of Godement–Jacquet FE (valid for matrix coefficients, not Whittaker functions); correct tool is the JPSS local Rankin–Selberg functional equation / $\gamma$-factor.

**Recommended path:** single-shell homogeneity collapse (q2-7) with $V$ = newvector of $\pi$, the shift $u_Q$ matching the level; compute $c_0$ honestly via Matringe newvector Whittaker values (q2-8); replace any GJ step by the JPSS functional equation (q2-2); verify the toy case $n=1$ ($\mathrm{GL}_2\times\mathrm{GL}_1$) first.
